zh.lifeSummary: 當Nicolas II de Lalaing, Seigneur de Lalaing於大約1225年出生在比利時海瑙特時，他的父親Simon II de Lalaing, Seigneur de Lalaing43歲，母親Alexandrine de Lesdaing27歲。他於1274年與Agnes de Willerval結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他於1270年登記服兵役。他去世於1285年享年61歲，埋葬在法國諾爾德拉萊艾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Nicolas II de Lalaing, Seigneur de Lalaing于大约1225年出生在比利时海瑙特时，他的父亲Simon II de Lalaing, Seigneur de Lalaing43岁，母亲Alexandrine de Lesdaing27岁。他于1274年与Agnes de Willerval结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他于1270年登记服兵役。他去世于1285年享年61岁，埋葬在法国諾爾德拉萊艾。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Nicolas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Nicholas
meanings: form of the post-classical Greek personal name Nikolaos, derived from nikē ‘victory’ + laos ‘people’. The spelling with -ch- first occurred as early as the 12th century, and became firmly established at the time of the Reformation, although Nicolas is still occasionally found. St Nicholas was a 4th-century bishop of Myra in Lycia, about whom virtually nothing factual is known, although a vast body of legend grew up around him, and he became the patron saint of Greece and of Russia, as well as of children, sailors, merchants, and pawnbrokers. His feast day is 6 December , and among the many roles which legend has assigned to him is that of bringer of Christmas presents, in the guise of ‘Santa Claus’ (an alteration of the Dutch form of his name, Sinterklaas).

variants.name: Nicolao
meanings: form of the post-classical Greek personal name Nikolaos, derived from nikē ‘victory’ + laos ‘people’. The spelling with -ch- first occurred as early as the 12th century, and became firmly established at the time of the Reformation, although Nicolas is still occasionally found. St Nicholas was a 4th-century bishop of Myra in Lycia, about whom virtually nothing factual is known, although a vast body of legend grew up around him, and he became the patron saint of Greece and of Russia, as well as of children, sailors, merchants, and pawnbrokers. His feast day is 6 December , and among the many roles which legend has assigned to him is that of bringer of Christmas presents, in the guise of ‘Santa Claus’ (an alteration of the Dutch form of his name, Sinterklaas).

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Nicolas II de Lalaing, Seigneur de Lalaing was born about 1225, in Hainaut, Belgium, his father, Simon II de Lalaing, Seigneur de Lalaing, was 43 and his mother, Alexandrine de Lesdaing, was 27. He married Agnes de Willerval in 1274. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. He registered for military service in 1270. He died in 1285, at the age of 61, and was buried in Lallaing, Nord, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Nicolas II de Lalaing, Seigneur de Lalaing est né environ 1225, à Hainaut, Belgique, son père, Simon II de Lalaing, Seigneur de Lalaing, avait 43 ans et sa mère, Alexandrine de Lesdaing, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Agnes de Willerval en 1274. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 3 filles. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1270. Il est décédé en 1285, à l’âge de 61 ans, et a été enterré à Lallaing, Nord, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Nicolas II de Lalaing, Seigneur de Lalaing um 1225 in Hennegau, Belgien geboren wurde, war sein Vater Simon II de Lalaing, Seigneur de Lalaing 43 und seine Mutter Alexandrine de Lesdaing 27. Er hat 1274 Agnes de Willerval geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er hat sich 1270 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. Er starb 1285 im Alter von 61 Jahren und wurde in Lallaing, Nord, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Nicolas II de Lalaing, Seigneur de Lalaing è nato il circa 1225 nel Hainaut, Belgio, il suo padre, Simon II de Lalaing, Seigneur de Lalaing, aveva 43 anni e il suo madre, Alexandrine de Lesdaing, aveva 27 anni. Lui sposato Agnes de Willerval nel 1274. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 3 figlie. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1270. Morì nel 1285, all'età di 61 anni, e fu sepolto a Lallaing, Nord, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Nicolas II de Lalaing, Seigneur de Lalaingが約1225年にベルギーヘイナトで生まれた時，父Simon II de Lalaing, Seigneur de Lalaingは43歳，母Alexandrine de Lesdaingは27歳であった。1274年にAgnes de Willervalと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1270年に兵役登録をした。彼は1285年に61歳で死去し，フランスノードラレイングにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Nicolas II de Lalaing, Seigneur de Lalaing(이)가 대략 1225년 벨기에 하인오트에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Simon II de Lalaing, Seigneur de Lalaing(은)는 43세였고, 그의 어머니 Alexandrine de Lesdaing(은)는 27세였다. 그는 1274년 Agnes de Willerval(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1270년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 그는 1285년 61세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 노르드 라레이그에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Nicolas II de Lalaing, Seigneur de Lalaing nasceu aproximadamente 1225, em Hainaut, Bélgica, seu pai, Simon II de Lalaing, Seigneur de Lalaing, tinha 43 anos e sua mãe, Alexandrine de Lesdaing, tinha 27 anos. Ele casou-se com Agnes de Willerval em 1274. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1270. Ele faleceu em 1285, com 61 anos, e foi sepultado em Lallaing, Nord, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Nicolas II de Lalaing, Seigneur de Lalaing родился около 1225 г. в местности ханаут, Бельгия, его отцу по имени Simon II de Lalaing, Seigneur de Lalaing было 43 года, а матери, которую звали Alexandrine de Lesdaing, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Agnes de Willerval в 1274 г. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери. В 1270 году он поступил на военную службу. Он умер в 1285 г. в возрасте 61 год и был похоронен в местности лаллеинг, норд, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Nicolas II de Lalaing, Seigneur de Lalaing nació aproximadamente 1225, en Hainaut, Bélgica, su papá, Simon II de Lalaing, Seigneur de Lalaing, tenía 43 años y su mamá, Alexandrine de Lesdaing, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Agnes de Willerval en 1274. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 3 hijas. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1270. Murió en 1285, a la edad de 61 años, y fue sepultado en Lallaing, Nord, Francia.
